steps.title: Translate the 4% annual increase
steps.content: The initial population is $15{,}000$, so the coefficient should be $15{,}000$. A $4\%$ yearly increase means each year is multiplied by $1+0.04=1.04$.

steps.title: Build the exponential model
steps.content:
After $x$ years, the factor $1.04$ is applied $x$ times:
$$p=15{,}000(1.04)^x$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content: The equations $p=0.96{ \left(15,000\right)}^{x}$ and $p=1.04{ \left(15,000\right)}^{x}$ put $15{,}000$ in the exponential base and misuse the initial population. The equation $p=15,000{ \left(0.96\right)}^{x}$ uses $0.96$, which would be a decrease. The model $p=15,000{ \left(1.04\right)}^{x}$ is correct.
